notes: Golden retrieval eval. Scoring looks at the ACTIVITY context memory.retrieve() returns — items of type 'fact' and 'episode'. Prior Q&A comes back as type 'assist' (reference only) and is never part of the scored set, so listing an assist- label in must_not_retrieve asserts it stayed out of activity retrieval. Today's raw events are returned unconditionally and carry no label, so they are not scored.

cases.question: what have I been reading about sqlite fts5 bm25 ranking?
cases.notes: The regression case: six manual-assist episodes are the newest rows and match these terms hard, so they used to take most of the 8 episode slots and pushed the one real browsing episode out.

cases.question: what have I been doing with sqlite ranking, stripe webhooks and pdf invoices?
cases.notes: Crowd-out proof: a broad query where six prior Q&A episodes take six of the eight episode slots, pushing real coding activity off the end of the list. Recall here is 2/3 before the fix.

cases.question: what did I work on in the invoicer project?
cases.notes: Two coding sessions on the second project, on different days.

cases.question: when was I looking at stripe webhooks?
cases.notes: Browsing docs must win over the answer PIOS gave about the same topic.

cases.question: did I get an email about an overdue invoice?
cases.notes: Outlook titles only; PIOS never reads message bodies.

cases.question: what have I done on the pios project?
cases.notes: Multi-day recall across three sessions on one project.

cases.question: which projects am I coding on in Visual Studio Code?
cases.notes: Consolidated facts must surface, not just episodes.

cases.question: tax rates
cases.notes: Bare keyword query, no question framing.
